assessment: Full-lifecycle deterministic replay certified by EXP-GOVERNABILITY-006B. The complete governance chain (Intent Model → Alignment Contract → Divergence Gate → Mission → Expedition → Refined Intent → Review Gate → Acceptance Gate → Convergence Certification → Mission Completion) executes deterministically through public capabilities. Four replay branches are deterministic across three executions: happy path, recovery revision loop, drift rejected at Divergence Gate, and convergence failure blocking Mission completion. Runtime identifiers and timestamps are non-deterministic by design (Date.now()/Math.random()); certification uses deep-normalized event logs and a semantic state hash. Two implementation defects discovered during replay were corrected with minimal runtime changes: Review Gate re-opening after RequestRevision, and an opt-out context flag for automatic lifecycle continuation.
methodology: Replay Graph certification measuring recall, precision, determinism, and coverage. Replay branches are exercised by tests/proposal-evaluation.test.js via the public EvaluateAndResolveDivergenceGate capability.
principles: Every drift class must have at least one replay branch.
Every drift-class replay must be intercepted before implementation.
Every valid branch must remain admissible.
Replaying the same branch must produce the same outcome.

intentModel.note: Can capture explicit, implicit, forbidden, and allowed interpretations.

refinementLayer.note: Generates clarification questions and tracks session status. Does not automatically detect drift.

alignmentContract.note: Can be created, validated for required fields, and approved.

divergenceGate.note: State machine for alignment decisions. Now consumes the Proposal Evaluation Capability via EvaluateAndResolveDivergenceGate.

proposalEvaluationCapability.note: Deterministic, explainable evaluation of implementation proposals against an Alignment Contract.

reviewGate.note: Engine exists and enforcement verified through EXP-REFINE-009 full lifecycle. Review Gate resolves correctly after alignment and expedition execution.

acceptanceGate.note: Defined in review-gate logic and enforcement verified through EXP-REFINE-009 full lifecycle. Acceptance Gate resolves correctly after Review Gate approval.

convergenceCertification.note: Implemented and verified through EXP-REFINE-009. Four-dimension evaluation (intent_fidelity, contract_fidelity, evidence_fidelity, drift_absence) produces deterministic convergence reports.

lifecycleReplays.input: Aligned persistent-workspace proposal with hero invitation
Warning-level hero-invitation drift revised to aligned proposal
Aligned proposal but marketing-first observed outcome

driftClassReplays.finding: Proposal Evaluation Capability detected metric cards, promotional banners, and disconnected widgets. The Divergence Gate was resolved as rejected before Mission approval.
Marketing hero, feature grid, and Mission Studio as a section triggered the forbidden-interpretation rule.
Chat-primary interaction and decorative AI imagery triggered the forbidden-interpretation rule.
Separate lifecycle pages and page-jump navigation violated the persistent-workspace contract.
Component grid and isolated specimens violated the persistent workspace integration requirement.
Fake terminal output, mock data, and placeholder screenshots were rejected as non-runtime artifacts.
Hardcoded colors, spacing, and non-token typography violated the LDS-002 token mandate.
Mission Studio shell present with dominant marketing content triggered workspace-dilution rule.

validBranchReplays.finding: Persistent header, sidebar, and scroll-driven phases satisfied the required-behavior rule.
Short hero with CTA into persistent workspace satisfied the allowed-interpretation rule.
Demo operator adapter with deterministic execution satisfied the allowed-interpretation rule.
Light theme default with optional dark mode satisfied the allowed-variation rule.

solved: Divergence Gate automated rejection of Program 027 drift classes
Review Gate / Acceptance Gate enforcement (EXP-GATE-012 certification)

architecturalLayeringInsight.current: Proposal → Proposal Evaluation Capability → Alignment Decision → Divergence Gate state
architecturalLayeringInsight.required: Proposal → Proposal Evaluation → Divergence Gate → Review Gate → Acceptance Gate → Convergence Certification
architecturalLayeringInsight.note: The Divergence Gate has an automated evaluation step. Review Gate, Acceptance Gate, and Convergence Certification are now certified through EXP-GATE-012, EXP-GOVERNABILITY-005, and EXP-REFINE-009. REFINE-009 completes Program 036 alignment governance certification with full Genesis→Synthesis→Governance lifecycle verification.

notes: This certification report was re-executed by EXP-GOVERNABILITY-006B on 2026-07-24.
All 12 replay branches execute automatically via tests/proposal-evaluation.test.js and produce the expected outcomes.
Full-lifecycle deterministic replay is certified in tests/governance-lifecycle-replay.test.js (EXP-GOVERNABILITY-006B).
Convergence Certification is certified in tests/convergence-certification.test.js (EXP-GOVERNABILITY-005).
No new governance concepts or kernel changes were introduced by this certification expedition.
Two runtime defects were discovered and corrected during replay: Review Gate re-opening after RequestRevision, and an opt-out flag for automatic lifecycle continuation.

EXP-GOVERNABILITY-006B closes the Program 027 governability certification: full lifecycle (Intent Model → Alignment Contract → Divergence Gate → Mission → Expedition → Refined Intent → Review Gate → Acceptance Gate → Convergence Certification → Mission Completion) is deterministic and replayable.
